recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — phone filming RCA monitor. Same lab as V25. Similar close framing — task area fills most of frame. 1280x720 at 30fps (lower resolution than V25's 1920x1080). Dark/light suture visible. Moderate debris on perforated board. Stryker tower visible at right. FLS Fundamentals binder below monitor.

frame_analyses.description: Drain centered on pink foam block on white perforated base. No instruments. Same RCA monitor lab setup as V25.
Instruments entering from lower right. One instrument tip visible at drain area with suture/needle. Hand visible at lower left holding instrument outside trainer. TIMING STARTS ~6s.
Both instruments at drain area. Needle visible with suture extending downward from instrument. Active needle driving through drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Already driving needle at 15s — fast placement.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments crossed in X pattern over drain. Suture placed. Transitioning to first throw wrapping. Suture ends extending from drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement complete ~22-24s (~17s from first instrument). Very fast placement — near series best.

frame_analyses.description: First throw wrapping. Both instruments at drain. Suture loop visible hanging below drain. Active wrapping in progress.
Instruments spread wide laterally. Suture lines extending from drain to both instruments. Tightening the first throw — classic lateral pull pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw tightening at 45s. Throw 1 took ~20s (25-45s). Good lateral technique.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw wrapping. Both instruments at drain with suture loops visible. One instrument from lower right, one from lower left. Active wrapping.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw at 55s. Fast transition from first throw.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw in progress. Instruments at drain center. Suture wrapping visible. One instrument from upper right pulling suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw complete ~60s (~13s). Third throw wrapping at 65s.

frame_analyses.description: Suture tails extending from knot at drain center. Instrument from upper right — transitioning to cutting or just started cutting. Tails visible extending upward and to right.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw complete ~72-74s (~12s). Cutting beginning. All three throws in ~48s — fastest in recent series.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting in progress. Instrument at drain from upper area. Suture tail visible extending downward from knot. One tail may already be cut.
Still cutting. Long suture tails visible extending upward from drain — arcing above the field. Blue/green instrument from lower right. Appears to be cutting the second tail.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: ~22s into cutting. The long tail arc suggests the second tail is being positioned for final cut.

frame_analyses.description: Task complete. Instruments withdrawn. Drain visible with knot at center — compact knot with short-to-moderate tails. Drain slit appears well-closed. No obvious gap visible at this resolution. Drain not avulsed. Clean final result.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completion at ~100s. Total task time from first instrument: ~94s. NEW ALL-TIME BEST.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 24-46s (22s). Surgeon's knot assumed. Good lateral tightening visible at 45s.
Third throw 60-74s (14s). Symmetric with throw 2. Good efficiency.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Marks not independently visible on RCA monitor. Placement area appears correct. Conservative 2mm estimate per mark.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: At 101s, drain slit appears well-closed with compact knot. Short-to-moderate tails. No obvious gap at this resolution. Confidence low due to monitor recording. Drain not avulsed.

technique_summary: V26 at approximately 94 seconds is a NEW ALL-TIME BEST — smashing V14's 107s record by 13 seconds. Every phase was executed at or near series-best pace: placement ~16s (near best), throws totaling ~50s (best since V14's 48s), and cutting at ~26s (NEW SERIES BEST by a wide margin — V14's previous best was 29s, V24's breakthrough was 40s). The cutting improvement is the headline: from V25's regressed 67s to 26s represents a 61% reduction and the first time cutting has dropped below 30s since V14. This is the first sub-100s performance in the entire 26-video series. FLS score of ~500 would be the highest ever recorded. The trainee has clearly consolidated V24's gains and pushed through to a new performance tier. At 94s, this approaches competitive FLS examination territory.
improvement_suggestions: CELEBRATE: First sub-100s performance. NEW ALL-TIME BEST by 13 seconds.
Cutting at ~26s is the most dramatic single-phase improvement in the series — from 67s (V25) to 26s.
Placement at 16s is excellent but could potentially shave 2-3s with pre-staged needle loading.
First throw at 22s is the longest phase proportionally — investigate if the surgeon's knot double-wrap is the bottleneck.
RECORDING: Still RCA monitor at 720p. Direct camera would confirm this extraordinary result with higher confidence.
Verify this time estimate carefully — if confirmed, the trainee has entered a qualitatively different performance tier.
Continue this session rhythm — whatever warmup/preparation led to V26 should be replicated.
strengths: NEW ALL-TIME BEST: ~94 seconds — 13s faster than V14 (107s), 23s faster than V24 (117s)
FIRST SUB-100 SECOND PERFORMANCE in the entire 26-video series
CUTTING BREAKTHROUGH CONSOLIDATED: ~26s — series best (previous: 29s V14, 40s V24, 67s V25)
Throws at ~50s total — second best ever (V14: 48s)
ALL PHASES at or near personal bests simultaneously
Drain appears well-closed, not avulsed — 26 consecutive videos without avulsion
Validates the training feedback loop — cutting drills and consistency work paying off

phase_comparison.placement_16s: Near series best. V24: 18s. V25: 22s. V14: 22s.
phase_comparison.throws_50s: Second best ever. V14: 48s. V24: 56s. V25: 58s.
phase_comparison.cutting_26s: NEW SERIES BEST. V14: 29s. V24: 40s. V25: 67s. MASSIVE improvement.

key_observations: 94s / ~500 FLS — NEW ALL-TIME BEST. First sub-100s. Beats V14 (107s) by 13 seconds.
CUTTING: 26s — 61% improvement from V25 (67s). Best ever by 3s (V14: 29s).
Throws: 50s — only V14 (48s) was faster. All three throws efficient and symmetric.
V25→V26: 150s→94s = 56-second improvement. The V25 regression was NOT a trend — it was a dip before a massive breakthrough.
The pattern: V24 breakthrough (117s) → V25 regression (150s) → V26 NEW PEAK (94s). Two steps forward, one step back, three steps forward.
5-video trajectory: V22 152 → V23 155 → V24 117 → V25 150 → V26 94. Exponential improvement envelope.
If confirmed, this performance is firmly in FLS examination passing range.
26th video: the full training arc — 198s baseline → 218s crash → 107s peak → 150s plateau → 94s new peak — is a textbook mastery curve.
